test_cases.title: Valid user sees inventory after login
test_cases.description: Happy path: a registered, non-locked user signs in with correct credentials and reaches the inventory list.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC is about the user journey through the UI — they submit the form and land on the inventory page. The journey crosses page boundaries; an API check alone would not validate that the redirect happens and the inventory grid renders. Also serves as the positive baseline against which TC-002's negative case diverges.
preconditions: A non-locked Saucedemo user exists (standard_user / secret_sauce — documented on the login screen).
expected_results: URL changes to a path ending in /inventory.html.
At least one inventory item is visible on the page.

test_cases.title: Invalid password is rejected with a clear error
test_cases.description: Negative case: the user submits a wrong password. The form rejects the credentials, the URL stays on the login page, and an error banner appears.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC explicitly says the user remains on the login page AND sees an error message. The 'sees an error' part is a UI assertion that belongs at E2E. The 'no partial session' guarantee is mostly a server-side concern, but Saucedemo has no backend so we assert the observable UI fact (no navigation, error visible).

steps.action: Fill the Password field with a wrong value.

expected_results: URL remains at the login page (path is '/' or ends in '/').

An error banner is visible whose copy includes the words 'username' and 'password' do not match (Saucedemo's actual error wording: 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service').

test_cases.title: Locked account is rejected at the API boundary
test_cases.description: Permission check: when a locked account submits valid credentials, the auth endpoint must refuse the request and not create a session. The UI's error display is a separate concern; this case asserts the server-side guarantee.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The AC is about session creation, not visual feedback. Permission/role checks belong at the API per docs/automation-decision-model.md; testing only via UI would let an API-level bypass slip through. NOTE: Saucedemo has no real auth endpoint, so this case will execute against the reqres.in equivalent in Phase 1.5 (see docs/ambiguities.md A3). In Phase 1 the case is recorded but not executed.
preconditions: A locked Saucedemo-equivalent user exists (locked_out_user / secret_sauce in Saucedemo terms; the actual Phase 1.5 target is reqres.in).

steps.action: POST credentials for the locked account to the auth endpoint.

expected_results: Response status code is in the 4xx range (typically 400 or 401).
Response body does not contain a session token or auth cookie.
No session cookie is set on the response.

test_cases.title: Visual review of login error styling
test_cases.description: Subjective check that the error banner is legible and on-brand. Captured for manual execution; not automated.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The check is about visual quality — colour, contrast, copy readability — which automated assertions cannot judge reliably. Marked manual so it still appears on the QA checklist but does not bloat the automation suite.
preconditions: A Saucedemo user account is available.

steps.action: Trigger an invalid-credentials error and inspect the banner visually.

expected_results: The banner uses an error colour and is readable against the page background.
The copy is specific enough that a non-technical user knows what to do next.
